text:
HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
METASTATIC BREAST CANCER - DISEASE STATUS/TREATMENT RESPONSE ASSESSMENT

**CHIEF COMPLAINT**
Ms. Marisol Fuentes-Delgado is a 59-year-old Hispanic woman with metastatic triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) presenting for follow-up after restaging imaging showing partial response to ongoing first-line metastatic therapy with sacituzumab govitecan (Trodelvy) plus ola (Lynparza). She is approximately 4 months into this regimen and we are reviewing imaging results, toxicity profile, symptom management, and ongoing treatment plan.

**HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS**
Ms. Fuentes-Delgado carries a complex oncologic history that is reviewed briefly here for context. She was initially diagnosed with stage IIB (T2N1M0) triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast at age 58, with a grade 3, Ki-67 92% tumor, lymphovascular invasion present, and a germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC). Her tumor was also HRD-high (HRD score 62, SBS3 dominant mutational signature) and PD-L1 positive (CPS 28).

She received neoadjuvant pembro (Keytruda) plus dose-dense AC for 4 cycles followed by weekly pacli for 12 weeks (with Keytruda throughout), then underwent left modified radical mastectomy with bilateral prophylactic mastectomy given her BRCA1 mutation. Surgical pathology demonstrated a mixed pathologic response — residual invasive ductal carcinoma measuring 1.8 cm, one positive sentinel lymph node (micro-metastasis), and an RCB class II designation. She subsequently received post-mastectomy radiation therapy (PMRT) to the left chest wall and regional nodes (50.4 Gy with a 10 Gy boost), followed by adjuvant cape (dose-adjusted to 850 mg/m2 BID and subsequently reduced further to 750 mg/m2 BID due to grade 2 hand-foot syndrome) plus adjuvant Keytruda continuation.

Approximately 14 months post-surgery, surveillance imaging revealed new hepatic lesions (right hepatic lobe segments VI and VII) and a lytic right 8th rib lesion, confirmed by CT-guided liver biopsy showing metastatic TNBC (GATA3+, ER-/PR-/HER2- maintained). Repeat tumor NGS at the metastatic site identified a new acquired AKT1 p.E17K mutation and CCND1 amplification in addition to the known BRCA1 biallelic loss and TP53 p.R248W, suggesting clonal evolution. HRD score remained high at 68, TMB 11 mut/Mb, MSS. UGT1A1 genotyping at initiation of Trodelvy revealed homozygous *28/*28 status.

Given prior anthracycline (ddAC), taxane (pacli), and fluoropyrimidine (Xeloda) exposure in the neoadjuvant/adjuvant setting, and the patient's germline BRCA1 mutation with maintained HRD-high status, she was initiated on sacituzumab govitecan (SG) at a reduced starting dose of 7.5 mg/kg (then further reduced to 5 mg/kg after cycle 2 grade 4 febrile neutropenia hospitalization) plus Lynparza 300 mg orally twice daily as first-line metastatic therapy in the context of a clinical trial/expanded access framework.

Today's visit: The patient presents for review of restaging CT imaging obtained after 3 cycles of SG/ola. The scan demonstrated a meaningful reduction in the segment VI hepatic lesion (from 1.4 cm down to 0.7 cm), possible complete response of the segment VII lesion (now below reliable measurement threshold), and stability of the right rib lytic lesion without new osseous lesions. No new sites of disease were identified on imaging — a very encouraging result.

Interval since last visit: Patient has now received a total of 4 complete cycles of SG/ola (cycles 3 and 4 at 5 mg/kg SG dose with day 2 pegfilgrastim [Neulasta] prophylaxis). She reports manageable but persistent fatigue. She continues to have mild nausea predominantly on days 1-3 of each ola cycle (grade 1-2), which she manages with ondansetron PRN. She denies worsening neuropathy. Right rib pain continues to be present but manageable with scheduled acetaminophen and intermittent ibuprofen; she is not currently using opioid analgesics. No new febrile episodes since the hospitalization after cycle 2.

**REVIEW OF SYSTEMS**
- Constitutional: Grade 2 fatigue, ongoing. Appetite decreased but maintaining adequate oral intake. Weight stable (within 1 kg of prior visit). No fever, chills, or night sweats.
- HEENT: No new headaches, no vision changes.
- Pulmonary: No cough (prior radiation pneumonitis has resolved; confirmed on imaging). No dyspnea at rest; mild exertional dyspnea with moderate activity (NYHA class I-II).
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema. LVEF was mildly reduced at 58% on most recent echocardiogram (post-op). Cardiology surveillance ongoing.
- GI: Grade 1 diarrhea (1-2 loose stools per day, manageable with dietary modification and PRN loperamide). Mild oral discomfort, no frank mucositis. No nausea at rest; some nausea days 1-3 post-treatment.
- GU: No hematuria, no dysuria. Urinary frequency stable. Baseline CrCl ~49 mL/min (CKD-EPI; chronic hypertensive nephropathy and diabetic nephropathy risk).
- MSK/Neurologic: Residual peripheral neuropathy grade 1 — mild tingling in fingertips only, stable, not worsening on current regimen. Right posterior chest wall/rib aching, rated 3-4/10 at rest, 5-6/10 with activity, managed with analgesics as above.
- Skin/integument: No current hand-foot syndrome (resolved after cape discontinuation). No new rash. Bilateral mastectomy healing well; tissue expanders in place.
- Endocrine: Hypothyroidism — on levothyroxine 88 mcg daily, last TSH not yet rechecked this visit.
- Psychiatric: Patient reports moderate anxiety and depressive symptoms, now engaged with oncology social work and psychiatry (referral placed at prior visit). Initiated SSRI (sertraline 50 mg daily per psychiatry recommendation). Endorses improved mood and engagement with coping strategies. No suicidal ideation.

**PHYSICAL EXAMINATION**
General: Alert, oriented, pleasant but visibly fatigued woman in no acute distress. Well-nourished, mildly obese (BMI ~32.8). Wearing a wig (post-chemotherapy alopecia; hair regrowing slowly).
Vital signs: Blood pressure 130/82 mmHg (left arm), Heart rate 74 bpm, Respiratory rate 16/min, Temperature 36.8°C, SpO2 97% on room air, Weight 87.4 kg.
HEENT: Anicteric sclerae. Oropharynx: no mucositis, no thrush, no oral ulcerations. No lymphadenopathy in the cervical or supraclavicular chains.
Chest/Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No S3/S4 or murmur appreciated. Bilateral mastectomy scars well-healed; tissue expanders palpable bilaterally, soft, no overlying skin changes or erythema, no wound breakdown. Left chest wall with mild post-radiation skin hyperpigmentation without active desquamation. No palpable right axillary, supraclavicular, or infraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheeze, rales, or diminished breath sounds.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly appreciated on palpation (liver edge not palpable below right costal margin). No splenomegaly. No ascites clinically.
Musculoskeletal: Right posterior chest wall tenderness to palpation at approximately the 8th rib posterolaterally — consistent with known lytic rib metastasis. No visible swelling. Range of motion of bilateral shoulders and upper extremities maintained. No lower extremity edema.
Neurologic: Alert, cranial nerves intact. Motor strength 5/5 throughout. Sensation: mildly diminished light touch at fingertips bilaterally (grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, stable). Gait steady and unassisted.
Skin: No palmar-plantar erythema or skin peeling (prior hand-foot syndrome resolved). No rash consistent with immune toxicity.

**LABORATORY RESULTS**
Complete Blood Count (obtained day 1 of cycle 4, pre-treatment):
- WBC: 3.8 x10^9/L (ANC 2.1 x10^9/L — improved with prophylactic pegfilgrastim)
- Hemoglobin: 9.4 g/dL (grade 2 anemia; stable from cycle 3)
- Platelets: 187 x10^9/L
Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Creatinine: 1.18 mg/dL (CrCl estimated 48 mL/min by CKD-EPI; stable)
- ALT: 28 U/L, AST: 31 U/L, Total bilirubin: 0.9 mg/dL (normal liver function; no hepatotoxicity)
- Albumin: 3.6 g/dL
- Glucose (fasting): 134 mg/dL
HbA1c (from 2 weeks prior): 7.8% (slightly increased from 7.2% at adjuvant phase; endocrinology co-managing)
TSH (pending from today's draw — levothyroxine 88 mcg, last TSH was borderline elevated at 5.6 mU/L)
Tumor markers (not routinely used to guide treatment; obtained for trending):
- CA 27-29: 68 U/mL (elevated above normal; trending compared to prior values; note — in isolation not used for treatment decisions)
- CA 15-3: 72 U/mL (elevated; consistent with metastatic disease; trending)

**IMAGING RESULTS**
Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (obtained after 3 cycles of SG/ola, results reviewed today in detail with the patient):

The scan shows a gratifying response to treatment. The target right hepatic lobe lesion in segment VI has shrunk from 1.4 cm to 0.7 cm — that is a roughly 50% reduction in just 3 cycles, which is a very meaningful response. The second hepatic lesion in segment VII, which was 0.8 cm at the time of diagnosis of metastatic disease, is now essentially undetectable — there may still be a faint focus there but it is below the threshold where we can measure it reliably; it may be in complete response at that site. There are no new liver lesions. Lung fields are clear — the ground-glass opacities we saw with radiation pneumonitis last year have completely resolved, and there are no new nodules or masses. There is no mediastinal or axillary lymphadenopathy. The right posterior 8th rib lytic lesion remains stable in size at approximately 1.5 cm — it has not grown, and critically there are no new bony lesions anywhere. The left chest wall shows the expected post-surgical and post-radiation changes without any imaging features concerning for local recurrence.

Overall, this is a meaningful partial response. The liver lesions — the soft tissue disease — are responding well. The bone disease is stable. This is the best-case scenario we were hoping for at this interval assessment.

**ASSESSMENT AND PLAN**

1. Metastatic triple-negative breast cancer (ypT1cN1mi at surgery → metastatic recurrence ~14 months post-op), germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant, HRD-high, PD-L1 CPS 28, MSS, TMB 11 mut/Mb. Acquired AKT1 E17K at metastatic site.

   - Imaging demonstrates partial response to sacituzumab govitecan (Trodelvy) 5 mg/kg plus ola (Lynparza) 300 mg BID: right hepatic lesion reduced ~50%, segment VII lesion possibly in complete response, rib lesion stable, no new sites of disease. This is the optimal response we could hope for at this point in treatment, and we are very pleased with these results.
   - Continue current regimen: SG 5 mg/kg days 1 and 8 of a 21-day cycle (dose maintained at current reduction given UGT1A1 *28/*28 genotype and prior severe neutropenia), plus ola 300 mg BID continuously.
   - Pegfilgrastim (Neulasta) 6 mg subcutaneously day 2 of each cycle — continue indefinitely given UGT1A1 homozygous status and ANC nadir history.
   - Monitor CBC with differential on day 1 of each cycle and mid-cycle (day 10-12) to catch ANC nadirs.
   - Next restaging CT planned after cycle 6 (approximately 2 months from today).
   - Bone scan to be considered at next restaging interval given known rib lesion; will reassess with radiation oncology whether palliative radiation to the rib lesion is warranted if pain escalates or lesion shows progression.
   - Discussed AKT1 E17K mutation identified at metastatic site — this is a potentially targetable alteration (capivasertib is now approved in combination with fulvestrant for ER+ disease; for TNBC the utility is less established). Continue monitoring; clinical trial options including AKT inhibitor combinations will be considered at progression.

2. Anemia (grade 2, Hgb 9.4 g/dL) — likely multifactorial (myelosuppression from SG, chronic disease, poor oral iron absorption).
   - Check iron studies and ferritin; if iron-deficient, initiate IV iron supplementation (ferric carboxymaltose or ferumoxytol).
   - Transfusion threshold set at Hgb < 7.5 g/dL per institutional policy in metastatic setting.
   - No erythropoiesis-stimulating agent given metastatic solid tumor context.

3. Right rib pain from lytic metastasis — currently grade 2-3, managed with acetaminophen 1000 mg TID scheduled plus ibuprofen PRN.
   - Patient continues to decline opioids; fully informed of options including low-dose short-acting oxycodone for breakthrough pain.
   - Radiation oncology referral placed for consideration of palliative radiation to right 8th rib lesion if pain becomes less controllable or if lesion progresses.
   - Palliative care team engaged — following for symptom management and goals-of-care support. Continue palliative care co-management.
   - Consider bone-modifying agent: given confirmed osseous metastatic disease, zoledronic acid (Zometa) 4 mg IV every 4 weeks (renally adjusted for CrCl ~48 mL/min — dose at standard 4 mg but infusion duration extended to 30 minutes; dental clearance to be obtained first).

4. Hypothyroidism (Keytruda-induced) — on levothyroxine 88 mcg daily.
   - TSH drawn today; if TSH remains elevated, will increase levothyroxine to 100 mcg and recheck in 6-8 weeks.
   - Endocrinology continues to co-manage.

5. Type 2 diabetes mellitus — HbA1c 7.8% (slight worsening from 7.2%).
   - Metformin dose optimization limited by renal function (CrCl ~48 mL/min; metformin generally held when CrCl < 45 mL/min; currently borderline — continue monitoring).
   - Discussed with endocrinology; potential addition of a GLP-1 receptor agonist or SGLT2 inhibitor (with caution given renal function) under their guidance.
   - Reinforce dietary counseling.

6. Cardiac surveillance — LVEF 58% on last echo (mild reduction from baseline 62% after anthracycline-containing neoadjuvant regimen).
   - Repeat echocardiogram ordered — has not been performed in approximately 6 months.
   - Cardiology co-management ongoing; no clinical heart failure symptoms.
   - Will reassess cardiotoxicity risk longitudinally; Lynparza and sacituzumab govitecan are not traditionally considered cardiotoxic agents.

7. Psychiatric/psychosocial — patient now engaged with psychiatry and oncology social work, on sertraline 50 mg daily (psychiatry prescribed). Reports modest improvement in mood and coping.
   - Continue current support structure. Encourage participation in support group (TNBC Foundation, BRCA sister organizations).
   - Discussed BRCA1 hereditary implications for family members; patient aware; genetic counseling resources reiterated.

8. Peripheral neuropathy — grade 1 (stable, fingertip tingling only); not progressing on current Lynparza/SG regimen.
   - Duloxetine 30 mg daily continued for residual neuropathic symptoms.
   - No dose modification to current regimen warranted for neuropathy at this time.

9. Hypertension — blood pressure 130/82 mmHg today; well-controlled on lisinopril. Continue current dose. Monitor BP at each visit.

10. General supportive care and surveillance:
    - Nutritional support consultation to address fatigue, oral intake, and diabetes management.
    - Physical therapy referral for deconditioning and right rib pain/mobility.
    - Patient to call with any fever > 38.0°C (given myelosuppression risk), worsening abdominal pain, new shortness of breath, or neurological changes.
    - Comprehensive medication reconciliation performed today. Current active medications: sacituzumab govitecan 5 mg/kg IV (days 1 and 8, q21d), ola 300 mg PO BID, pegfilgrastim 6 mg SQ day 2, levothyroxine 88 mcg PO daily, lisinopril 20 mg PO daily, metformin 1000 mg PO BID (hold if CrCl drops below 45), duloxetine 30 mg PO daily, sertraline 50 mg PO daily, acetaminophen 1000 mg PO TID (scheduled), ondansetron 8 mg PO PRN nausea, loperamide 2 mg PO PRN diarrhea.

**RETURN VISIT**: In 3 weeks for cycle 5, day 1 of sacituzumab govitecan; labs and exam at that time. Next full disease restaging imaging after cycle 6 (approximately 6-8 weeks from today).

Time spent with patient today: approximately 45 minutes, greater than 50% counseling and coordination of care.

Electronically signed.
[Attending Oncologist, Medical Oncology / Hematology-Oncology]
